description: Figure 8 comprises three line-and-marker subplots comparing hyperparameter optimization strategies for the Extra Trees regression model applied to yield strength prediction in medium-manganese low-density steels. Subplot (a) plots RMSE versus optimization method: Grid Search-ET yields 80.239, Bayesian-ET yields 80.721, and the proposed GA-ET (labeled "Our") sharply reduces the error to 31.6 MPa. Subplot (b) plots MAPE: Grid Search-ET is 11.037, Bayesian-ET is 11.423, and GA-ET drops to 4.39%. Subplot (c) plots R²: Grid Search-ET is 0.972, Bayesian-ET is 0.976, and GA-ET rises to 0.997. The consistent trend across all three metrics demonstrates that Genetic Algorithm optimization substantially outperforms both grid search and Bayesian optimization, minimizing prediction error while maximizing the coefficient of determination. This figure supports the core conclusion that GA-optimized Extra Trees is the most suitable modeling approach for accurate, data-driven yield strength prediction in this alloy system.
